---
title: "About"
---

My background combines geoscience, geography, and environmental analysis, with a focus on GIS, hydrology, and spatial data workflows.

I earned a B.A. in Geoscience and a B.A. in History from the University of Connecticut, followed by an M.S. in Geography from Penn State University. That combination shaped how I approach environmental questions: with both technical rigor and an interest in the broader spatial and regional context behind them.

My work centers on using GIS and data-driven methods to examine how environmental systems vary across space and time, especially in relation to streamflow, flood behavior, and watershed dynamics.

---

## How I Approach GIS

I think of GIS as more than map production. For me, it is a way to organize environmental data, connect spatial and temporal patterns, and communicate results clearly.

That usually means:

- organizing and integrating environmental datasets  
- building reproducible spatial workflows  
- analyzing patterns across multiple spatial scales  
- translating technical outputs into clear visuals and applied interpretation  

---

## Technical Background

- **GIS & Mapping:** ArcGIS Pro, ArcGIS Online, QGIS  
- **Analysis & Programming:** R, spatial analysis, statistical analysis, environmental data workflows  
- **Common Data Sources:** USGS streamflow records, climate datasets, watershed boundaries  
- **Other Tools:** GitHub, Quarto  

---

## Areas of Interest

I am especially interested in work involving:

- water resources and hydrologic systems  
- environmental and infrastructure planning  
- geospatial workflow design and automation  
- applied spatial problem-solving  

---

## Looking Ahead

I am seeking opportunities where I can apply GIS and geospatial analysis to practical environmental and infrastructure challenges, especially in roles that value strong workflows, spatial reasoning, and clear communication.
